knowledge_text: AZ refers to state = 'AZ'; stars < 3;
gold_sql: SELECT COUNT(business_id) FROM Business WHERE state LIKE 'AZ' AND stars < 3
column_value_notes: `Business`.`stars`: declared REAL; 15585/15585 non-NULL; 9 distinct; range 1.0 to 5.0; sample values: '4.0', '3.5', '4.5'.
`Business`.`state`: declared TEXT; 15585/15585 non-NULL; 3 distinct; range AZ to SC; sample values: 'AZ', 'CA', 'SC'.
schema_linking_notes: Result shape: count.
Relevant tables: Business, Business_Attributes, Business_Categories, Business_Hours, Reviews, Checkins.

knowledge_text: Average cost = AVG(StandardCost)
gold_sql: SELECT AVG(T2.StandardCost) FROM Product AS T1 INNER JOIN ProductCostHistory AS T2 ON T1.ProductID = T2.ProductID WHERE T1.ProductNumber = 'CA-1098'

knowledge_text: open 24 hours refers to attribute_name = 'Open 24 Hours' AND attribute_value = 'true';
gold_sql: SELECT COUNT(T2.business_id) FROM Attributes AS T1 INNER JOIN Business_Attributes AS T2 ON T1.attribute_id = T2.attribute_id WHERE T1.attribute_name LIKE 'Open 24 Hours' AND T2.attribute_value LIKE 'TRUE'

knowledge_text: Product with more than 5 quantity refers to Quantity>5
gold_sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM Product AS T1 INNER JOIN ShoppingCartItem AS T2 ON T1.ProductID = T2.ProductID WHERE T2.Quantity > 5
column_value_notes: `PhoneNumberType`.`Name`: declared TEXT; 3/3 non-NULL; 3 distinct; range Cell to Work; sample values: 'Cell', 'Home', 'Work'.
`Product`.`Name`: declared TEXT; 504/504 non-NULL; 504 distinct; range AWC Logo Cap to Women's Tights, S; sample values: 'AWC Logo Cap', 'Adjustable Race', 'All-Purpose Bike Stand'.

Relevant tables: PhoneNumberType, Product, AddressType, ContactType, CountryRegion, Culture.

knowledge_text: kind of wi-fi refers to attribute_value where attribute_name = 'Wi-Fi'; business No. refers to business_id;
gold_sql: SELECT T2.attribute_value FROM Attributes AS T1 INNER JOIN Business_Attributes AS T2 ON T1.attribute_id = T2.attribute_id WHERE T2.business_id = 10172 AND T1.attribute_name LIKE 'wi-fi'

knowledge_text: buffets refers to category_name = 'Buffets'; gyms refers to category_name = 'Gyms'; difference = SUBTRACT(SUM(category_name = 'Buffets'), SUM(category_name = 'Gyms'));
gold_sql: SELECT SUM(CASE WHEN T1.category_name LIKE 'Buffets' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) - SUM(CASE WHEN T1.category_name LIKE 'Gyms' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) FROM Categories AS T1 INNER JOIN Business_Categories AS T2 ON T1.category_id = T2.category_id

knowledge_text: High quality refers to the product's quality class, therefore Class = 'H'; the lowest transacted quantity refers to Quantity = 1
gold_sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM Product AS T1 INNER JOIN TransactionHistory AS T2 ON T1.ProductID = T2.ProductID WHERE T1.Class = 'H' ORDER BY T2.Quantity ASC LIMIT 1
column_value_notes: `Product`.`Class`: declared TEXT; 247/504 non-NULL; 3 distinct; range H to M; sample values: 'L', 'H', 'M'.
`ProductInventory`.`Quantity`: declared INTEGER; 1069/1069 non-NULL; 343 distinct; range 0 to 924; sample values: '99', '81', '324'.
schema_linking_notes: Result shape: minimum.
Relevant tables: Product, ProductInventory, ShoppingCartItem, TransactionHistory, TransactionHistoryArchive.

Relevant columns: Product.Class, ProductInventory.Quantity, ShoppingCartItem.Quantity, TransactionHistory.Quantity, TransactionHistoryArchive.Quantity, Product.Color, Product.DaysToManufacture, Product.DiscontinuedDate, Product.FinishedGoodsFlag, Product.ListPrice, Product.MakeFlag, Product.ModifiedDate, Product.Name, Product.ProductID, Product.ProductLine, Product.ProductModelID.

knowledge_text: business category refers to category_name; most 5 star reviews refers to MAX(COUNT(category_name WHERE star_reviews = 5));
gold_sql: SELECT T1.category_name FROM Categories AS T1 INNER JOIN Business_Categories AS T2 ON T1.category_id = T2.category_id INNER JOIN Business AS T3 ON T2.business_id = T3.business_id INNER JOIN Reviews AS T4 ON T3.business_id = T4.business_id WHERE T4.review_stars = 5 GROUP BY T1.category_name ORDER BY COUNT(T1.category_name) DESC LIMIT 1
column_value_notes: `Reviews`.`business_id`: declared INTEGER; sampled 10000 non-NULL values from 322906 rows; 463 distinct values in sample; primary key; joins `Business`.`None`; range 1 to 99; sample values: '184', '24', '56'.
`Business`.`review_count`: declared TEXT; 15585/15585 non-NULL; 4 distinct; range High to Uber; sample values: 'Low', 'Medium', 'High'.
schema_linking_notes: Result shape: count.
Relevant tables: Reviews, Business_Categories, Business_Attributes, Business, Business_Hours, Categories.

Relevant columns: Reviews.business_id, Business_Categories.business_id, Business_Categories.category_id, Business_Categories.category_id, Business_Attributes.business_id, Business_Attributes.business_id, Business.business_id, Business.review_count, Business_Hours.business_id, Categories.category_id, Categories.category_name, Checkins.business_id, Tips.business_id, Users.user_review_count, Reviews.review_length, Reviews.review_stars.

knowledge_text: The Mountain line refers to the product line, therefore ProductLine = 'M'
gold_sql: SELECT COUNT(T2.TransactionID) FROM Product AS T1 INNER JOIN TransactionHistory AS T2 ON T1.ProductID = T2.ProductID WHERE T1.ProductLine = 'M'
column_value_notes: `Product`.`ProductLine`: declared TEXT; 278/504 non-NULL; 4 distinct; range M to T; sample values: 'R', 'M', 'T'.
`PhoneNumberType`.`Name`: declared TEXT; 3/3 non-NULL; 3 distinct; range Cell to Work; sample values: 'Cell', 'Home', 'Work'.

knowledge_text: year the user join the Yelp refers to user_yelping_since_year; star reviews refers to review_stars;
gold_sql: SELECT T2.user_yelping_since_year FROM Reviews AS T1 INNER JOIN Users AS T2 ON T1.user_id = T2.user_id WHERE T1.review_stars = 5 GROUP BY T2.user_yelping_since_year ORDER BY COUNT(T1.review_stars) DESC LIMIT 1
column_value_notes: `Reviews`.`user_id`: declared INTEGER; sampled 10000 non-NULL values from 322906 rows; 7023 distinct values in sample; primary key; joins `Users`.`None`; range 10009 to 9995; sample values: '4732', '30855', '16810'.
`Business`.`stars`: declared REAL; 15585/15585 non-NULL; 9 distinct; range 1.0 to 5.0; sample values: '4.0', '3.5', '4.5'.

knowledge_text: Sales profit = MULTIPLY(SUBTRACT(ListPrice, StandardCost; Quantity)), where ShoppingCartID = '20621'
gold_sql: SELECT SUM((T1.ListPrice - T1.StandardCost) * T2.Quantity) FROM Product AS T1 INNER JOIN ShoppingCartItem AS T2 ON T1.ProductID = T2.ProductID WHERE T2.ShoppingCartID = 20621
column_value_notes: `ShoppingCartItem`.`ShoppingCartID`: declared TEXT; 3/3 non-NULL; 2 distinct; range 14951 to 20621; sample values: '20621', '14951'; text numeric values may need CAST(REPLACE(..., ',', '') AS REAL).
`Product`.`ListPrice`: declared REAL; 504/504 non-NULL; 103 distinct; range 0.0 to 3578.27; sample values: '0.0', '337.22', '782.99'.
schema_linking_notes: Result shape: sum.
Relevant tables: Product, ShoppingCartItem, ProductCostHistory, ProductInventory, ProductListPriceHistory, TransactionHistory.

knowledge_text: long reviews refers to review_length = 'long'; most number of long reviews refers to MAX(COUNT(review_length = 'long')); average ratings = AVG(review_stars);
gold_sql: SELECT CAST(SUM(T1.review_stars) AS REAL) / COUNT(T1.review_stars) FROM Reviews AS T1 INNER JOIN Users AS T2 ON T1.user_id = T2.user_id WHERE T1.review_length LIKE 'Long' GROUP BY T1.user_id ORDER BY COUNT(T1.review_length) DESC LIMIT 1
column_value_notes: `Reviews`.`review_length`: declared TEXT; sampled 10000 non-NULL values from 322906 rows; 3 distinct values in sample; range Long to Short; sample values: 'Medium', 'Long', 'Short'.
`Tips`.`tip_length`: declared TEXT; sampled 10000 non-NULL values from 87157 rows; 3 distinct values in sample; range Long to Short; sample values: 'Medium', 'Short', 'Long'.
schema_linking_notes: Result shape: count.
Relevant tables: Users, Reviews, Tips, Users_Compliments, Business, Days.

Relevant columns: Users.user_average_stars, Users.user_review_count, Reviews.review_length, Reviews.review_stars, Tips.tip_length, Users_Compliments.number_of_compliments, Business.review_count, Users.user_fans, Users.user_id, Users.user_votes_cool, Users.user_votes_funny, Users.user_votes_useful, Users.user_yelping_since_year, Reviews.review_votes_cool, Reviews.review_votes_funny, Reviews.review_votes_useful.

knowledge_text: High quality refers to the product's quality class, therefore Class = 'H'
gold_sql: SELECT Name, ListPrice FROM Product WHERE Class = 'H'
column_value_notes: `PhoneNumberType`.`Name`: declared TEXT; 3/3 non-NULL; 3 distinct; range Cell to Work; sample values: 'Cell', 'Home', 'Work'.
`Product`.`Class`: declared TEXT; 247/504 non-NULL; 3 distinct; range H to M; sample values: 'L', 'H', 'M'.

knowledge_text: long reviews refers to review_length = 'long'; most number of long reviews refers to MAX(COUNT(review_length = 'long')); category refers to category_name;
gold_sql: SELECT T4.category_name FROM Reviews AS T1 INNER JOIN Business AS T2 ON T1.business_id = T2.business_id INNER JOIN Business_Categories AS T3 ON T2.business_id = T3.business_id INNER JOIN Categories AS T4 ON T3.category_id = T4.category_id WHERE T1.review_length LIKE 'Long' GROUP BY T2.business_id ORDER BY COUNT(T1.review_length) DESC LIMIT 1
column_value_notes: `Reviews`.`review_length`: declared TEXT; sampled 10000 non-NULL values from 322906 rows; 3 distinct values in sample; range Long to Short; sample values: 'Medium', 'Long', 'Short'.
`Tips`.`tip_length`: declared TEXT; sampled 10000 non-NULL values from 87157 rows; 3 distinct values in sample; range Long to Short; sample values: 'Medium', 'Short', 'Long'.
schema_linking_notes: Result shape: count.
Relevant tables: Reviews, Business, Business_Categories, Tips, Business_Attributes, Categories.

knowledge_text: short tips refers to tip_length = 'short'; most number of short tips refers to MAX(COUNT(tip_length = 'short')); category refers to category_name;
gold_sql: SELECT DISTINCT T1.category_name FROM Categories AS T1 INNER JOIN Business_Categories AS T2 ON T1.category_id = T2.category_id INNER JOIN Business AS T3 ON T2.business_id = T3.business_id INNER JOIN Tips AS T4 ON T3.business_id = T4.business_id WHERE T4.tip_length LIKE 'short'
column_value_notes: `Tips`.`tip_length`: declared TEXT; sampled 10000 non-NULL values from 87157 rows; 3 distinct values in sample; range Long to Short; sample values: 'Medium', 'Short', 'Long'.
`Reviews`.`review_length`: declared TEXT; sampled 10000 non-NULL values from 322906 rows; 3 distinct values in sample; range Long to Short; sample values: 'Medium', 'Long', 'Short'.

knowledge_text: year the user join the Yelp refers to user_yelping_since_year; most number of short tips refers to MAX(COUNT(tip_length = 'Short'));
gold_sql: SELECT T2.user_yelping_since_year FROM Tips AS T1 INNER JOIN Users AS T2 ON T1.user_id = T2.user_id WHERE T1.tip_length LIKE 'short' GROUP BY T2.user_yelping_since_year ORDER BY COUNT(T1.tip_length) DESC LIMIT 1
column_value_notes: `Tips`.`tip_length`: declared TEXT; sampled 10000 non-NULL values from 87157 rows; 3 distinct values in sample; range Long to Short; sample values: 'Medium', 'Short', 'Long'.
`Reviews`.`review_length`: declared TEXT; sampled 10000 non-NULL values from 322906 rows; 3 distinct values in sample; range Long to Short; sample values: 'Medium', 'Long', 'Short'.

Product with the lowest rating refers to the rating

reviewer where Rating = 1

knowledge_text: user No. refers to user_id; short tip refers to tip_length = 'short'; category refers to category_name;
gold_sql: SELECT T4.category_name FROM Tips AS T1 INNER JOIN Business AS T2 ON T1.business_id = T2.business_id INNER JOIN Business_Categories AS T3 ON T2.business_id = T3.business_id INNER JOIN Categories AS T4 ON T3.category_id = T4.category_id WHERE T1.user_id = 70271

knowledge_text: The Road line refers to the product line, therefore ProductLine = 'R'
gold_sql: SELECT T1.Name, T2.ReviewerName, T2.Rating, T2.Comments FROM Product AS T1 INNER JOIN ProductReview AS T2 USING (productID) WHERE T1.ProductLine = 'R'
column_value_notes: `PhoneNumberType`.`Name`: declared TEXT; 3/3 non-NULL; 3 distinct; range Cell to Work; sample values: 'Cell', 'Home', 'Work'.
`Product`.`Name`: declared TEXT; 504/504 non-NULL; 504 distinct; range AWC Logo Cap to Women's Tights, S; sample values: 'AWC Logo Cap', 'Adjustable Race', 'All-Purpose Bike Stand'.
schema_linking_notes: Result shape: select.
Relevant tables: Product, PhoneNumberType, ProductReview, AddressType, ContactType, CountryRegion.

Relevant columns: Product.Name, Product.ProductLine, PhoneNumberType.Name, ProductReview.Comments, ProductReview.Rating, AddressType.Name, ContactType.Name, CountryRegion.Name, Culture.Name, Currency.Name, Department.Name, Location.Name, ProductCategory.Name, ProductModel.Name, ProductSubcategory.Name, SalesReason.Name.

knowledge_text: automotive businesses refers to category_name = 'Automotive'; percentage = MULTIPLY(DIVIDE(SUM(category_name = 'Automotive'), COUNT(business_id)), 1.0);
gold_sql: SELECT CAST(SUM(CASE WHEN T2.category_name LIKE 'Automotive' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS REAL) * 100 / COUNT(T1.business_id) AS "percentage" FROM Business_Categories AS T1 INNER JOIN Categories AS T2 ON T1.category_id = T2.category_id
column_value_notes: `Business`.`business_id`: declared INTEGER; 15585/15585 non-NULL; 15585 distinct; primary key; range 1 to 15585; sample values: '1', '2', '3'.
`Business`.`review_count`: declared TEXT; 15585/15585 non-NULL; 4 distinct; range High to Uber; sample values: 'Low', 'Medium', 'High'.
schema_linking_notes: Result shape: count.

knowledge_text: Women's clothing refers to category_name = 'Women''s Clothing'; Men's clothing refers to category_name = 'Men''s Clothing'; percentage more = MULTIPLY(DIVIDE(SUBTRACT(SUM(category_name = 'Women''s Clothing'), SUM(category_name = 'Men''s Clothing')), COUNT(business_id)), 1.0);
gold_sql: SELECT CAST(SUM(CASE WHEN T2.category_name LIKE 'Women''s Clothing' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS REAL) * 100 / COUNT(T1.business_id) - CAST(SUM(CASE WHEN T2.category_name LIKE 'Men''s Clothing' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS REAL) * 100 / COUNT(T1.business_id) AS "more percentage" FROM Business_Categories AS T1 INNER JOIN Categories AS T2 ON T1.category_id = T2.category_id
column_value_notes: `Business`.`business_id`: declared INTEGER; 15585/15585 non-NULL; 15585 distinct; primary key; range 1 to 15585; sample values: '1', '2', '3'.
`Business`.`review_count`: declared TEXT; 15585/15585 non-NULL; 4 distinct; range High to Uber; sample values: 'Low', 'Medium', 'High'.
schema_linking_notes: Result shape: count.
Relevant tables: Reviews, Business_Categories, Business_Attributes, Business, Business_Hours, Categories.

knowledge_text: Product without any rejected quantity refers to RejectedQty = 0
gold_sql: SELECT T1.Name, T2.UnitPrice FROM Product AS T1 INNER JOIN PurchaseOrderDetail AS T2 ON T1.ProductID = T2.ProductID WHERE T2.RejectedQty = 0 ORDER BY T2.LineTotal DESC LIMIT 1

knowledge_text: closed/not running refers to active = 'False'; most reviews refers to MAX(COUNT(user_id));
gold_sql: SELECT T1.business_id FROM Business AS T1 INNER JOIN Reviews AS T2 ON T1.business_id = T2.business_id WHERE T1.city LIKE 'Sun City' AND T1.active LIKE 'FALSE' GROUP BY T1.business_id ORDER BY COUNT(T2.review_length) DESC LIMIT 1
column_value_notes: `Business`.`active`: declared TEXT; 15585/15585 non-NULL; 2 distinct; range false to true; sample values: 'true', 'false'.

`Reviews`.`business_id`: declared INTEGER; sampled 10000 non-NULL values from 322906 rows; 463 distinct values in sample; primary key; joins `Business`.`None`; range 1 to 99; sample values: '184', '24', '56'.
schema_linking_notes: Result shape: count.

CREATE TABLE "Address"
(

        primary key autoincrement,

)

knowledge_text: Profit = SUBTRACT(ListPrice, StandardCost); the highest rating in review refers to Rating = 5
gold_sql: SELECT T1.Name, T1.ListPrice - T1.StandardCost FROM Product AS T1 INNER JOIN ProductReview AS T2 ON T1.ProductID = T2.ProductID ORDER BY T2.Rating DESC LIMIT 1
